Hero.subtitle: Capture every call on your Mac. Ask AI across the full record later.
Hero.desc: Reki is a local-first meeting memory for people who live in Zoom, Meet, Teams, Slack huddles, and in-person conversations.

Problem.title1: How many tools are you paying for
Problem.desc: Most teams patch together platform add-ons, standalone note takers, and manual exports just to keep meeting context. The result is extra spend, scattered transcripts, and no single place to ask what was decided.
Problem.beforeTotalDesc: You pay more and remember less
Problem.afterTitle: One local workspace for every conversation
Problem.afterDesc: Across Zoom, Meet, Teams, Slack huddles, and in-person conversations
afterItems: Capture calls and in-person discussions in one app
Keep audio and notes on your Mac
Ask AI across every transcript and summary
Use your own model keys instead of another markup
Problem.afterTotalDesc: Keep the record and query it later

Features.title2: once Reki is your memory layer

rows.title: You stop paying per meeting surface.
rows.body: Instead of buying a separate AI layer for every platform, you capture the conversations themselves. Reki gives you one local place for transcripts, summaries, and follow-up context.

rows.title: Scattered calls become searchable context.
rows.body: Every conversation on your Mac can land in the same archive. Once the record is unified, asking 'what did we decide?' becomes easier than hunting through apps, channels, and exports.

rows.title: Your team gets recall it can actually use.
rows.body: Past 1:1s, customer calls, and decision reviews stop disappearing into separate silos. Reki turns them into a working memory you can query whenever the why behind a decision matters.

Features.chatQuestion: What did we commit to in the Acme QBR last Thursday?
Features.chatAnswer:
Here are the 3 commitments:
1. Launch the pilot with the sales team first
2. Finalize admin permissions by next Tuesday
3. Send a redlined rollout plan after legal review

WorksWith.title: One recorder for the tools your team already uses.
WorksWith.desc: Because Reki captures audio at the OS level, you do not need a different note-taking flow for every platform. Install it once and keep the record in one place.

Compare.title1: Why teams pick a local-first recorder
Compare.desc: Reki is built around owning the raw conversation record, not renting it back from every meeting platform.
Compare.footnote: * Representative categories based on public pricing and product information

reki.text: All Mac apps and in-person in one flow

notes.text: Usually covers only major meeting apps
Often strong per meeting, weaker across all notes

facts: Audio files stay on your Mac
Transcripts go straight to the AI provider you choose
Summaries run on your own model keys
Privacy.providersDesc: Use the model providers you already pay for

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: We are over plan for the quarter. What actually moved the number?

transcript.speaker: Morgan
transcript.text: The biggest driver was conversion from the new outbound segment, especially after the onboarding change.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: Good. Pull the exact numbers into the board prep doc before Friday.

HowItWorks.calendarNote: Who joined, when it happened, and what it was about get linked to the recording automatically.

outputCards.title: Full-text search
outputCards.body: Search across people, dates, and what was actually said.

outputCards.title: AI Q&A
outputCards.body: Ask things like 'what did we decide last quarter?' in plain English.

outputCards.title: Accumulated context
outputCards.body: The more you use it, the more your conversations turn into usable working memory.

FinalCTA.title:
Own the record.
Query the past.
FinalCTA.desc: Start with the calls you already have today. Reki gives you one local archive, one search surface, and one cleaner way to remember what happened.
FinalCTA.successDesc: We will send access details as soon as your spot opens.
FinalCTA.errorGeneral: Something went wrong. Please try again later.

preview.heading: Home
preview.subheading: Every work conversation, in one place.

sessions.title: Board Prep Review / HIBACHI inc.
sessions.preview: Board memo needs exact pipeline numbers by Friday. Legal redline follows the pricing update. Hiring ask stays out of this draft.

sessions.title: Acme QBR / ACME
sessions.preview: Pilot starts with sales in month one. Admin permissions finalize next Tuesday. Procurement asked for a security appendix.

sessions.title: 1:1 / Alex
sessions.preview: Agreed to fix onboarding drop-off before expanding outbound hiring. Team bandwidth is the main constraint through June.

preview.aiQuestion: What did we decide with Acme about the pilot rollout?
